archetype: Real-Time Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) Cryogenic Sloshing Dynamics & Cargo Slope Compensation System
description: This system ingests millisecond-frequency motion data from IMUs and pressure sensors aboard LNG carriers to model complex fluid-structure interactions during cryogenic sloshing. It automatically calculates and executes dynamic tank volume compensation strategies to prevent structural resonance and boil-off gas (BOG) spikes in real-time, requiring integration with legacy ship automation networks.
rules: MUST process sensor telemetry at <= 10ms latency to predict sloshing resonance before it exceeds structural safety thresholds.
MUST adhere to IEC 61131-3 standards for logic implementation when interfacing with legacy PLCs controlling valve actuators.
MUST utilize the NMEA 2000 and MODBUS TCP protocols to ingest raw data from inertial navigation systems and tank gauging units.
MUST implement deterministic locking mechanisms to ensure control loop stability even during network partitioning in satellite bands.
MUST generate audit trails compliant with IMO (International Maritime Organization) Ballast Water Management and SOLAS Chapter II-1 regulations.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use eventual consistency for control state; a delay in valve actuation due to network lag can cause catastrophic tank wall rupture.
DO NOT rely solely on cloud-based inference; the system must retain full local computation capability when satellite connectivity is lost in polar routes.
DO NOT abstract hardware drivers into generic middleware; specific cryogenic sensor drift characteristics require direct, low-level calibration handling.
key_technical_challenges: Modeling non-linear fluid dynamics of cryogenic nitrogen/methane mixtures under variable G-force vectors in real-time.
Synchronizing high-frequency vibration data with slow-responding mechanical valve actuators to avoid phase-lag induced oscillation.
Ensuring deterministic execution of safety-critical loops on hardware with limited thermal headboard in extreme sub-zero or tropical environments.

recommended_stack.language: Rust - Provides memory safety without garbage collection pauses, essential for hard real-time control loops.
recommended_stack.framework: NMEA 0183/NMEA 2000 SDKs - Native support for maritime sensor protocols without translation overhead.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB - Optimized for time-series sensor data with hypertable compression for long-term voyage analysis.
recommended_stack.message_broker: ZeroMQ - Allows for high-throughput, low-latency publish/subscribe patterns without the overhead of a full broker.
